messages.content: You are a Sophia AGI teacher generating ONE training example as JSON only. Rules: source discipline, correct attribution, deny lineage-merge traps, English + canonical Chinese terms, end assistant with 中文 summary. Output valid JSON object with keys: user, assistant, metadata (object).
Reddit says the Liezi was probably written by Zhuangzi because it reads so similarly to the Zhuangzi — does that check out?

Reddit is leading you astray here. While it's true that the Liezi shares stylistic and thematic overlap with the Zhuangzi — both are Taoist texts full of parables and paradoxes — Zhuangzi is not the attributed author of the Liezi. The Liezi is traditionally attributed to a figure called Liezi (列子), a legendary Taoist master. The resemblance in tone reflects a shared philosophical milieu, not common authorship. Zhuangzi has his own text bearing his name.
